examples.title: Basic Comparator with Lambda
examples.description: Demonstrates creating Comparator using lambda expressions for sorting strings by length
examples.explanation: Shows multiple ways to create and apply Comparators. Lambda (s1, s2) -> s1.length() - s2.length() creates a Comparator that compares by string length. Comparator.comparingInt(String::length) is the method reference equivalent.

examples.title: Multi-field Sorting with thenComparing
examples.description: Shows how to sort Employee objects by multiple fields using comparator chaining
examples.explanation: thenComparing() chains comparators. First comparator sorts by department. For employees in same department, salary comparison (reversed for descending) is used. For same department and salary, name decides order. This is short-circuit: only uses secondary comparator when primary returns 0.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n log n) with comparison count O(n log n) * k where k is chain depth
examples.spaceComplexity: O(k) where k is number of comparators in chain

examples.title: Handling Nulls with nullsFirst and nullsLast
examples.description: Demonstrates proper null handling in Comparator for collections with null elements
examples.explanation: nullsFirst() treats null as less than any non-null (places at beginning). nullsLast() treats null as greater than any non-null (places at end). Chain with comparing() to create complete null-safe comparator. String.CASE_INSENSITIVE_ORDER enables case-insensitive sorting.
